heaviest_rainfall.status: The heaviest total rainfall occurred on 09 November 2025, dropping 47.89mm of rain over the 144.8 hour storm duration with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: A relentless rainstorm took place on 09 November 2025, it lasted 144.8 hours and it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 23 and it happened on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm around.  It's the first significant stormy weather event since 19 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 6 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C), set on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is no heatwaves at the moment.  This year has experienced 6 heatwavess, with an average of 1.9 per year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 18 July 2025, there have been only 62 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (199°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind gust recorded on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 3.4°C), outside temperature (down 3.0°C), and feels like temperature (down 2.3°C) and drier conditions in rainfall (down 0.1 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in pressure (down 5 mbar) and cloud levels (down 148 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in outside temperature (up 9.7°C), inside temperature (up 7.5°C), and inside humidity (up 9%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 147.5m higher than yesterday, 321.4m higher than the past week, 366.8m higher than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 623m to a maximum of 1932m, averaging 1403.5m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 1082.1m and 1036.7m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; temperature should remain cool. Outside; icy, freezing temperatures. Also, dry air conditions likely prevail and winds will be practically absent. Drizzle or light rain possible, mostly clear skies with high cloud, and light levels will be low during the day. Changeable conditions.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-2.2°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
